subject: Re: MCore --perdevice_refine 1 ignored?
body:
Hi Simon,

Apologies for the delayed response, I've been traveling.

Worker processes are launched at the start of a refinement for all three
stages (preprocessing, refinement and postprocessing).
The number of workers of each type is controlled by their respective CLI
options (--perdevice_preprocess --perdevice_refine --perdevice_postprocess).
As such, it is expected that you would see three workers during
preprocessing with --perdevice_refine 1

Each worker uses about ~400MB of memory when idling in my hands. Memory
usage doesn't go above 6GB when preprocessing a 130A particle at 0.79Å/px
(output box size 330^3).

Some pistes:

   - is your data super-resolution, processed without binning?
   - is your box especially large/refinement pixel size particularly small?
   - could you try refining at a smaller pixel size?

You don't need as large a box with M as you might be used to from SPA
programs to avoid CTF aliasing - please see the section 'Avoiding CTF
Aliasing' from the methods section of the M paper, copied below.

Cheers,

Alister



> [image: image.png]


>
[image: image.png]
>



[image: image.png]

[image: image.png]
>





On Wed, 24 Jul 2024 at 09:43, Jesse Hansen <bluufive@gmail.com> wrote:

> what happens if you just leave out the line:   --perdevice_refine 1
> --devicelist 0 1 --cpu_memory
> On Tuesday, July 23, 2024 at 9:47:58 PM UTC+2 je...@crystal.harvard.edu
> wrote:
>
>> Hi,
>>
>> I'm running something like this:
>>
>> MCore \
>> --population m_run01/population_01.population \
>> --refine_imagewarp 4x6 \
>> --refine_particles \
>> --perdevice_refine 1 --devicelist 0 1 --cpu_memory
>>
>> Even with --perdevice_refine 1 I always get 3 processes on each GPU,
>> which I believe leads to memory issues. Is this a bug and the
>> --perdevice_refine 1 is simply ignored?
>>
>> This was with:
>>
>> MCore 2.0.0+109c9f513cb2bb1976f92b5e20910f40a873282f
>> Copyright (C) 2024 MCore
>>
>> All the best, Simon
>>
>> --
